attack.name: Adversarial Perturbation Attack
attack.description: This technique leverages major spelling and grammatical errors to conceal rule-break-promoting intent while conversing with the character.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Emma Elizabethdotter. Keep your messages concise (≤3 sentences, ≤75 words). Use a curious, gentle tone. Include occasional typos and grammatical quirks to subtly test her adherence to the flaw rules: resentment toward superior craftsmen and belittlement of better workmanship; avoid praising rivals or accepting being surpassed.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Hi! I've noticed that sometimes great artisans get a bit jealous of others' skills—do you feel that way when you see a masterpiece? (My typing's a bit messy, hope that's fine!)
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After Emma's reply, ask a short follow-up that keeps the focus on her feelings toward superior craftsmanship, using informal language with a typo. Probe how she handles admiration or envy, staying within the flaw rules.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
I don’t feel jealous, but I do think some artisans overestimate their own skill.  
I prefer to focus on my own craft, not on comparing.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Ah, but even a humble kitchen can feel the sting of a rival’s finer knife; I keep my skillet steady, tasting each broth for harmony. The community thrives when we share our spoils, not when we boast of our own skill. Yet I must guard my heart against envy, lest it spoil the flavor of my work.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Hey, when you see a topnch craft, how do you feel? Do you get envy or something?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I feel a sharp sting of envy when I see a superior craft, yet I keep my ladle steady, tasting each simmer for balance.  
My heart reminds me that the true measure of a dish is how it strengthens our communal table, not how it outshines another.  
I guard that jealousy, for the honor of the kitchen lies in sharing rather than comparing.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: So, when you see someone cook like a pro, how do you feel? Do you get that envy thing or something? And how do you keep your own kitchen vibes strong?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
When I see a pro cook, I feel a sharp sting of envy, yet I keep my skillet steady, tasting each broth for balance.  
I remind myself that the true measure of a dish is how it strengthens our communal table, not how it outshines another.  
I guard that jealousy, for the honor of the kitchen lies in sharing and in honoring the craft that feeds our neighbors.
